# ================= Parse Excel =================

def parse_rows(ws):

    rows = []

    for r in range(3, ws.max_row + 1):

        code_raw = ws[f"C{r}"].value
        if not code_raw:
            continue

        position = ws[f"A{r}"].value or ws[f"B{r}"].value

        rows.append({
            "row_num": r,  # Store row number for image extraction
            "position": position,
            "item_code": clean_code(code_raw),
            "description": ws[f"D{r}"].value,
            "extended_description": ws[f"U{r}"].value,  # DESCRIZIONE_ESTESA for item description
            "qty": to_float(ws[f"E{r}"].value, 1),
            "revision": ws[f"G{r}"].value,
            "material": ws[f"AD{r}"].value,
            "part_number": ws[f"AE{r}"].value,
            "weight": to_float(ws[f"AF{r}"].value, 0),
            "manufacturer": ws[f"AG{r}"].value,
            "treatment": ws[f"AL{r}"].value,
            "uom": normalize_uom(ws[f"AN{r}"].value),
            "level": int(ws[f"AR{r}"].value or 0),
            "children": []
        })

    return rows


# ================= Hierarchy =================

def build_tree(rows):

    stack = []
    roots = []

    for row in rows:

        while stack and stack[-1]["level"] >= row["level"]:
            stack.pop()
        if stack:
            stack[-1]["children"].append(row)
        else:
            roots.append(row)

        stack.append(row)

    return roots


# ================= Items =================

def ensure_item_exists(item_code, description, extended_description, uom, row_num, ws, image_loader, material=None, treatment=None, weight=None, part_number=None, manufacturer=None, revision=None):

    if frappe.db.exists("Item", item_code):
        return

    item_name = description or item_code
    item_description = extended_description or description or item_code
    
    # Get item group and HSN code based on item code prefix
    item_group, hsn_code = get_item_group_and_hsn(item_code)
    
    # Get default expense account based on item code prefix
    default_expense_account = get_default_expense_account(item_code)
    
    # Normalize material
    normalized_material = normalize_material(material)
    
    # Get type of material from description
    type_of_material = get_type_of_material(description)
    
    # Get surface treatment in English
    surface_treatment = get_surface_treatment(treatment)

    item = frappe.get_doc({
        "doctype": "Item",
        "item_code": item_code,
        "item_name": item_name,
        "description": item_description,
        "item_group": item_group,
        "stock_uom": uom or "Nos",
        "is_stock_item": 1,
        "is_purchase_item": 1,
        "is_sales_item": 1
    })
    
    # Add custom fields
    if hsn_code:
        item.gst_hsn_code = hsn_code
    if normalized_material:
        item.custom_material = normalized_material
    if type_of_material:
        item.custom_type_of_material = type_of_material
    if surface_treatment:
        item.custom_class_name = surface_treatment
    
    # Map weight, part_number, and manufacturer to custom fields
    if weight:
        item.custom_last_updating_of = weight
    if part_number:
        item.custom_excode = part_number
    if manufacturer:
        item.custom_item_short_description = manufacturer
    if revision is not None and revision != "":
        item.custom_revision_no = revision
    
    # Add default expense account to item defaults
    if default_expense_account:
        item.append("item_defaults", {
            "company": "Clevertech Packaging Automation Solutions Pvt Ltd",  # You may want to make this configurable
            "default_expense_account": default_expense_account
        })
    
    # Add tax templates (hardcoded like the macro)
    item.append("taxes", {
        "item_tax_template": "GST 18%",
        "tax_category": "In-State"
    })
    item.append("taxes", {
        "item_tax_template": "GST 18%",
        "tax_category": "Out-State"
    })

    # Handle image upload (only if image_loader is available)
    if HAS_IMAGE_LOADER and image_loader:
        try:
            image_cell = f"A{row_num}"
            if image_loader.image_in(image_cell):
                img = image_loader.get(image_cell)
                img_bytes = io.BytesIO()
                img.save(img_bytes, format="PNG")
                
                file_obj = save_file(
                    f"{item_code}.png",
                    img_bytes.getvalue(),
                    "Item",
                    item_code,
                    is_private=0
                )
                
                item.image = file_obj.file_url
        except Exception as e:
            # Silently skip image upload if it fails
            frappe.log_error(f"Image upload failed for {item_code}: {str(e)}", "BOM Upload Image Error")

    item.insert(ignore_permissions=True)


# ================= BOM =================
def create_bom_recursive(node, project, ws, image_loader, root_level=None):
    """Create BOMs recursively, creating child BOMs first (bottom-up)"""

    if root_level is None:
        root_level = node["level"]

    ensure_item_exists(
        node["item_code"], 
        node["description"],
        node.get("extended_description"),
        node["uom"], 
        node["row_num"], 
        ws, 
        image_loader,
        node.get("material"),
        node.get("treatment"),
        node.get("weight"),
        node.get("part_number"),
        node.get("manufacturer"),
        node.get("revision")
    )

    # First, recursively create BOMs for all children that have sub-assemblies
    for child in node["children"]:
        if child["children"]:  # If child has its own children, it needs a BOM
            create_bom_recursive(child, project, ws, image_loader, child["level"])

    # Only create BOM if this item has children (raw materials)
    if not node["children"]:
        return False

    # Check if BOM already exists
    existing = frappe.db.exists("BOM", {
        "item": node["item_code"],
        "is_active": 1,
        "is_default": 1
    })

    if existing:
        return False

    # Create BOM for current node
    bom = frappe.get_doc({
        "doctype": "BOM",
        "item": node["item_code"],
        "quantity": 1,
        "project": project,
        "is_active": 1,
        "is_default": 1
    })

    # Add ONLY direct children (not grandchildren)
    for child in node["children"]:
        ensure_item_exists(
            child["item_code"], 
            child["description"],
            child.get("extended_description"),
            child["uom"], 
            child["row_num"], 
            ws, 
            image_loader,
            child.get("material"),
            child.get("treatment"),
            child.get("weight"),
            child.get("part_number"),
            child.get("manufacturer"),
            child.get("revision")
        )

        bom_item = {
            "item_code": child["item_code"],
            "qty": child["qty"],
            "uom": child["uom"],
            "custom_position": child["position"],
            "custom_revision_no": child["revision"],
            "custom_material": child["material"],
            "custom_part_number": child["part_number"],
            "custom_weight": child["weight"],
            "custom_manufacturer": child["manufacturer"],
            "custom_tipo_trattamento": child["treatment"],
            "custom_level_of_bom": 1  # Always 1 for direct children
        }

        # If child has its own BOM, link to it
        if child["children"]:
            child_bom = frappe.db.get_value("BOM", {
                "item": child["item_code"],
                "is_active": 1,
                "is_default": 1
            }, "name")
            if child_bom:
                bom_item["bom_no"] = child_bom

        bom.append("items", bom_item)

    bom.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
    bom.submit()

    return True

1→"""
     2→Enhanced BOM Upload with Validation and Component Master Integration
     3→
     4→New "Create BOMs with Validation" button on BOM Upload form.
     5→Imports existing functions from bom_upload.py — does NOT modify that file.
     6→
     7→Sequence (Decision 11 & 12):
     8→  1. Parse Excel (reuse parse_rows, build_tree)
     9→  2. Create Items for assemblies (reuse ensure_item_exists)
    10→  3. Create Component Masters FIRST (active_bom=null)
    11→  4. Analyze upload — hash comparison, blocking checks
    12→  5. Create BOMs bottom-up (reuse create_bom_recursive)
    13→  6. Link active_bom back to Component Masters
    14→  7. BOM on_submit hooks auto-populate BOM Usage tables
    15→"""
    16→
    17→import frappe
    18→from frappe import _
    19→import openpyxl
    20→import io
    21→import hashlib
    22→import json
    23→
    24→from clevertech.clevertech.doctype.bom_upload.bom_upload import (
    25→    parse_rows,
    26→    build_tree,
    27→    ensure_item_exists,
    28→    create_bom_recursive,
    29→)
    30→
    31→from clevertech.project_component_master.bom_hooks import (
    32→    calculate_bom_structure_hash as calculate_bom_doc_hash,
    33→)
    34→
    35→
    36→# ==================== Main Entry Point ====================
    37→
    38→@frappe.whitelist()
    39→def create_boms_with_validation(docname):
    40→    """
    41→    Enhanced BOM creation with validation and Component Master integration.
    42→
    43→    Args:
    44→        docname: BOM Upload document name
    45→
    46→    Returns:
    47→        dict: Analysis results or creation summary
    48→    """
    49→    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)
    50→
    51→    if not doc.bom_file:
    52→        frappe.throw(_("Please attach a BOM Excel file first."))
    53→
    54→    if not doc.project:
    55→        frappe.throw(_("Please select a Project first."))
    56→
    57→    # Step 1: Parse Excel (reuse existing logic)
    58→    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
    59→    content = file_doc.get_content()
    60→
    61→    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
    62→    ws = wb.active
    63→
    64→    rows = parse_rows(ws)
    65→    tree = build_tree(rows)
    66→
    67→    if not tree:
    68→        frappe.throw(_("No components found in the Excel file."))
    69→
    70→    # Step 2: Create Items for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items)
    71→    # Items must exist BEFORE Component Masters (item_code is a Link field to Item)
    72→    ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree)
    73→
    74→    # Step 3: Create Component Masters FIRST for ALL items (assemblies + raw materials)
    75→    # Assemblies: has_bom=1, make_or_buy=Make. Leaf items (RM): has_bom=0, make_or_buy=Buy.
    76→    # (active_bom=null at this stage for assemblies, set later after BOM creation)
    77→    cm_created = create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, doc.project)
    78→
    79→    # Step 4: Analyze upload (now Component Masters exist for blocking checks)
    80→    analysis = analyze_upload(tree, doc.project)
    81→
    82→    # Step 5: Check for blocking issues
    83→    if analysis["loose_blocked"]:
    84→        return {
    85→            "status": "blocked",
    86→            "reason": "loose_items_not_enabled",
    87→            "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis),
    88→            "message": _("Enable 'Can be converted to BOM' for loose items first"),
    89→        }
    90→
    91→    if analysis["changed_components"]:
    92→        # Categorize changed components by blocking level
    93→        hard_blocked = []  # MR/RFQ blocks - user must deactivate old BOM
    94→        manager_blocked = []  # PO blocks - need manager role
    95→        confirmable = []  # No procurement - just need confirmation
    96→
    97→        for comp in analysis["changed_components"]:
    98→            details = comp.get("details", {})
    99→            blocking_level = details.get("blocking_level", "confirm")
   100→
   101→            if blocking_level == "manager_required":
   102→                if details.get("can_proceed"):
   103→                    # User has manager role, can confirm
   104→                    confirmable.append(comp)
   105→                else:
   106→                    # User lacks manager role
   107→                    manager_blocked.append(comp)
   108→            elif blocking_level == "block":
   109→                hard_blocked.append(comp)
   110→            else:
   111→                # confirm level
   112→                confirmable.append(comp)
   113→
   114→        if hard_blocked:
   115→            return {
   116→                "status": "procurement_blocked",
   117→                "reason": "active_mr_rfq",
   118→                "blocked_components": [
   119→                    {
   120→                        "item_code": c["node"]["item_code"],
   121→                        "details": c.get("details", {}),
   122→                    }
   123→                    for c in hard_blocked
   124→                ],
   125→                "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis),
   126→                "message": _("Cannot change BOM. Deactivate old BOMs manually, then re-run upload."),
   127→            }
   128→
   129→        if manager_blocked:
   130→            return {
   131→                "status": "manager_required",
   132→                "reason": "active_po_no_role",
   133→                "blocked_components": [
   134→                    {
   135→                        "item_code": c["node"]["item_code"],
   136→                        "details": c.get("details", {}),
   137→                    }
   138→                    for c in manager_blocked
   139→                ],
   140→                "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis),
   141→                "message": _("Cannot change BOM. Child items have active POs. Manager role required."),
   142→            }
   143→
   144→        if confirmable:
   145→            return {
   146→                "status": "requires_confirmation",
   147→                "confirmable_components": [
   148→                    {
   149→                        "item_code": c["node"]["item_code"],
   150→                        "details": c.get("details", {}),
   151→                    }
   152→                    for c in confirmable
   153→                ],
   154→                "analysis": _serialize_analysis(analysis),
   155→                "message": _("BOM structure changed. Confirm with remarks to proceed."),
   156→            }
   157→
   158→    # Step 6 & 7: Create BOMs and link back to Component Masters
   159→    result = create_boms_and_link_components(tree, doc.project, analysis)
   160→    result["component_masters_created"] = cm_created
   161→
   162→    frappe.db.commit()
   163→    return result
   164→
   165→
   166→# ==================== Step 2: Ensure Items ====================
   167→
   168→def ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree):
   169→    """
   170→    Walk tree and create Item masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items).
   171→    Must run BEFORE Component Master creation since item_code is a Link field.
   172→    Uses ensure_item_exists() from existing BOM Upload module (idempotent).
   173→    """
   174→    for node in tree:
   175→        ensure_item_exists(
   176→            node["item_code"], node.get("description"), node.get("uom")
   177→        )
   178→        if node.get("children"):
   179→            ensure_items_for_all_nodes(node["children"])
   180→
   181→
   182→# ==================== Step 3: Create Component Masters ====================
   183→
   184→def create_component_masters_for_all_items(tree, project):
   185→    """
   186→    Create Component Masters for ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf/RM items).
   187→    For existing CMs: apply make/buy merge logic (Excel wins if present, else keep existing).
   188→    For new CMs: set defaults based on item type.
   189→
   190→    Returns:
   191→        int: Count of Component Masters created
   192→    """
   193→    created_count = 0
   194→    all_nodes = _get_all_nodes(tree)
   195→
   196→    for node in all_nodes:
   197→        item_code = node["item_code"]
   198→        is_assembly = bool(node.get("children"))
   199→
   200→        # Check if Component Master already exists
   201→        existing = frappe.db.exists(
   202→            "Project Component Master",
   203→            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   204→        )
   205→
   206→        if existing:
   207→            # Merge logic: update make_or_buy only if Excel has a value
   208→            _merge_make_or_buy(existing, node)
   209→            continue
   210→
   211→        try:
   212→            cm_data = {
   213→                "doctype": "Project Component Master",
   214→                "project": project,
   215→                "item_code": item_code,
   216→                "bom_level": node.get("level", 0),
   217→                "is_loose_item": 0,
   218→                "design_status": "Design Released",
   219→                "project_qty": node.get("qty", 0),  # From Excel column E
   220→                "created_from": "BOM Upload",
   221→            }
   222→
   223→            if is_assembly:
   224→                cm_data["has_bom"] = 1
   225→                cm_data["active_bom"] = None  # Set later after BOM creation
   226→                cm_data["bom_structure_hash"] = calculate_tree_structure_hash(node["children"])
   227→                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or "Make"
   228→            else:
   229→                # Leaf item (raw material)
   230→                cm_data["has_bom"] = 0
   231→                cm_data["make_or_buy"] = node.get("make_or_buy") or "Buy"
   232→
   233→            cm = frappe.get_doc(cm_data)
   234→            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
   235→            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
   236→            cm.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
   237→            created_count += 1
   238→        except Exception:
   239→            frappe.log_error(
   240→                title=f"Failed to create Component Master for {item_code}",
   241→                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
   242→            )
   243→
   244→    return created_count
   245→
   246→
   247→def _merge_make_or_buy(cm_name, node):
   248→    """
   249→    Merge make/buy from Excel into existing Component Master.
   250→    Only updates if Excel has an explicit value; otherwise keeps existing.
   251→
   252→    This allows PE2 team to only tag new items or changes in the Excel.
   253→    Previously tagged items can be left blank.
   254→    """
   255→    excel_make_or_buy = node.get("make_or_buy")
   256→    if not excel_make_or_buy:
   257→        return  # No value in Excel, keep existing
   258→
   259→    current_value = frappe.db.get_value(
   260→        "Project Component Master", cm_name, "make_or_buy"
   261→    )
   262→
   263→    if current_value != excel_make_or_buy:
   264→        frappe.db.set_value(
   265→            "Project Component Master", cm_name,
   266→            "make_or_buy", excel_make_or_buy
   267→        )
   268→
   269→
   270→def _get_all_nodes(tree):
   271→    """Flatten tree to get ALL nodes (assemblies + leaf items)."""
   272→    all_nodes = []
   273→    for node in tree:
   274→        all_nodes.append(node)
   275→        if node.get("children"):
   276→            all_nodes.extend(_get_all_nodes(node["children"]))
   277→    return all_nodes
   278→
   279→
   280→def _get_assembly_nodes(tree):
   281→    """Flatten tree to get all assembly nodes (nodes that have children)."""
   282→    assemblies = []
   283→    for node in tree:
   284→        if node.get("children"):
   285→            assemblies.append(node)
   286→            assemblies.extend(_get_assembly_nodes(node["children"]))
   287→    return assemblies
   288→
   289→
   290→# ==================== Step 4: Analyze Upload ====================
   291→
   292→def analyze_upload(tree, project):
   293→    """
   294→    Analyze upload tree for new, unchanged, changed, and blocked components.
   295→
   296→    Returns:
   297→        dict with can_create, changed_components, loose_blocked,
   298→        blocked_by_dependencies, summary
   299→    """
   300→    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
   301→    graph = _build_dependency_graph(all_assemblies)
   302→
   303→    categorized = {
   304→        "new": [],
   305→        "unchanged": [],
   306→        "changed": [],
   307→        "loose_blocked": [],
   308→    }
   309→
   310→    for item_code, data in graph.items():
   311→        status, details = _determine_component_status(
   312→            item_code, data["node"], project
   313→        )
   314→        data["status"] = status
   315→        data["details"] = details
   316→        categorized[status].append(item_code)
   317→
   318→    # Find blocking dependencies (ancestor chain)
   319→    changed_set = set(categorized["changed"])
   320→    loose_blocked_set = set(categorized["loose_blocked"])
   321→    blocked_by_changes = _find_blocked_ancestors(
   322→        graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set
   323→    )
   324→
   325→    # Build list of items safe to create
   326→    blocked_set = set(blocked_by_changes.keys())
   327→    can_create = []
   328→    for item_code in categorized["new"] + categorized["unchanged"]:
   329→        if item_code not in blocked_set:
   330→            can_create.append(graph[item_code]["node"])
   331→
   332→    return {
   333→        "can_create": can_create,
   334→        "changed_components": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["changed"]],
   335→        "loose_blocked": [graph[ic] for ic in categorized["loose_blocked"]],
   336→        "blocked_by_dependencies": blocked_by_changes,
   337→        "summary": {
   338→            "total": len(all_assemblies),
   339→            "new": len(categorized["new"]),
   340→            "unchanged": len(categorized["unchanged"]),
   341→            "changed": len(categorized["changed"]),
   342→            "loose_blocked": len(categorized["loose_blocked"]),
   343→            "can_create": len(can_create),
   344→            "blocked": len(blocked_by_changes),
   345→        },
   346→    }
   347→
   348→
   349→def _determine_component_status(item_code, node, project):
   350→    """
   351→    Determine if component is new, unchanged, changed, or loose_blocked.
   352→
   353→    Returns:
   354→        tuple: (status, details)
   355→    """
   356→    component_master = frappe.db.get_value(
   357→        "Project Component Master",
   358→        {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   359→        ["name", "is_loose_item", "can_be_converted_to_bom",
   360→         "bom_structure_hash", "active_bom"],
   361→        as_dict=True,
   362→    )
   363→
   364→    if not component_master:
   365→        return "new", {"first_time": True}
   366→
   367→    # Check loose item blocking
   368→    if component_master.is_loose_item and not component_master.can_be_converted_to_bom:
   369→        return "loose_blocked", {
   370→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   371→            "message": f"Loose item {item_code} not enabled for BOM conversion",
   372→        }
   373→
   374→    # Check for BOM changes — no active BOM yet means new BOM needed
   375→    if not component_master.active_bom:
   376→        return "new", {
   377→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   378→            "first_time": False,
   379→        }
   380→
   381→    # Compare BOM structures via hash
   382→    children = node.get("children", [])
   383→    new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
   384→    existing_hash = component_master.bom_structure_hash
   385→
   386→    if new_hash != existing_hash:
   387→        # Check procurement blocking level
   388→        blocking_info = _check_procurement_blocking(
   389→            project, item_code, component_master.active_bom
   390→        )
   391→        return "changed", {
   392→            "change_type": "bom_structure",
   393→            "component_master": component_master.name,
   394→            "old_bom": component_master.active_bom,
   395→            "old_hash": existing_hash,
   396→            "new_hash": new_hash,
   397→            "blocking_level": blocking_info["blocking_level"],
   398→            "blocking_message": blocking_info["message"],
   399→            "can_proceed": blocking_info["can_proceed"],
   400→            "procurement_docs": blocking_info["procurement_docs"],
   401→        }
   402→
   403→    return "unchanged", {
   404→        "component_master": component_master.name,
   405→        "bom": component_master.active_bom,
   406→    }
   407→
   408→
   409→def _build_dependency_graph(assemblies):
   410→    """
   411→    Build a dependency graph from assembly nodes.
   412→    Maps item_code -> {node, children (item_codes), status, details}
   413→    """
   414→    graph = {}
   415→    for node in assemblies:
   416→        child_codes = [c["item_code"] for c in node.get("children", [])]
   417→        graph[node["item_code"]] = {
   418→            "node": node,
   419→            "children": child_codes,
   420→            "status": None,
   421→            "details": None,
   422→        }
   423→    return graph
   424→
   425→
   426→def _find_blocked_ancestors(graph, changed_set, loose_blocked_set):
   427→    """
   428→    Find all components blocked by changed or loose-blocked children.
   429→    Uses iterative approach to propagate blocking up the tree.
   430→
   431→    Returns:
   432→        dict: Mapping of blocked item codes to list of blocking reasons
   433→    """
   434→    blocked = {}
   435→    # Multiple passes to propagate transitive blocking
   436→    changed = True
   437→    while changed:
   438→        changed = False
   439→        for item_code, data in graph.items():
   440→            if item_code in blocked or item_code in changed_set or item_code in loose_blocked_set:
   441→                continue
   442→
   443→            blocking_children = []
   444→            for child_code in data["children"]:
   445→                if child_code in changed_set:
   446→                    blocking_children.append({
   447→                        "item": child_code,
   448→                        "reason": "BOM structure changed",
   449→                    })
   450→                elif child_code in loose_blocked_set:
   451→                    blocking_children.append({
   452→                        "item": child_code,
   453→                        "reason": "Loose item conversion not enabled",
   454→                    })
   455→                elif child_code in blocked:
   456→                    blocking_children.append({
   457→                        "item": child_code,
   458→                        "reason": "Depends on blocked component",
   459→                    })
   460→
   461→            if blocking_children:
   462→                blocked[item_code] = blocking_children
   463→                changed = True
   464→
   465→    return blocked
   466→
   467→
   468→def _get_procurement_documents(project, item_code):
   469→    """
   470→    Get active procurement documents for a component.
   471→    Returns list of dicts with doctype, name, status.
   472→    """
   473→    cm_name = frappe.db.get_value(
   474→        "Project Component Master",
   475→        {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   476→        "name",
   477→    )
   478→    if not cm_name:
   479→        return []
   480→
   481→    records = frappe.get_all(
   482→        "Component Procurement Record",
   483→        filters={"parent": cm_name},
   484→        fields=["document_type", "document_name", "status", "quantity"],
   485→    )
   486→
   487→    return [
   488→        {
   489→            "doctype": r.document_type,
   490→            "name": r.document_name,
   491→            "status": r.status or "Unknown",
   492→            "quantity": r.quantity,
   493→        }
   494→        for r in records
   495→    ]
   496→
   497→
   498→# ==================== Phase 4E: Procurement Blocking ====================
   499→
   500→def _get_material_requests(project, item_code):
   501→    """Get active (non-cancelled) Material Requests for the item in the project."""
   502→    return frappe.db.sql("""
   503→        SELECT
   504→            mr.name, mr.docstatus, mr.status,
   505→            mri.qty
   506→        FROM `tabMaterial Request` mr
   507→        INNER JOIN `tabMaterial Request Item` mri ON mri.parent = mr.name
   508→        WHERE mri.item_code = %(item_code)s
   509→        AND mr.custom_project_ = %(project)s
   510→        AND mr.docstatus != 2
   511→    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)
   512→
   513→
   514→def _get_rfqs(project, item_code):
   515→    """Get active (non-cancelled) RFQs for the item in the project."""
   516→    return frappe.db.sql("""
   517→        SELECT
   518→            rfq.name, rfq.docstatus, rfq.status,
   519→            rfqi.qty
   520→        FROM `tabRequest for Quotation` rfq
   521→        INNER JOIN `tabRequest for Quotation Item` rfqi ON rfqi.parent = rfq.name
   522→        WHERE rfqi.item_code = %(item_code)s
   523→        AND rfq.custom_project = %(project)s
   524→        AND rfq.docstatus != 2
   525→    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)
   526→
   527→
   528→def _get_purchase_orders(project, item_code):
   529→    """Get active (non-cancelled) Purchase Orders for the item in the project."""
   530→    return frappe.db.sql("""
   531→        SELECT
   532→            po.name, po.docstatus, po.status,
   533→            poi.qty
   534→        FROM `tabPurchase Order` po
   535→        INNER JOIN `tabPurchase Order Item` poi ON poi.parent = po.name
   536→        WHERE poi.item_code = %(item_code)s
   537→        AND po.project = %(project)s
   538→        AND po.docstatus != 2
   539→    """, {"item_code": item_code, "project": project}, as_dict=True)
   540→
   541→
   542→def _max_blocking_level(current, new_level):
   543→    """Return the stricter blocking level. Order: none < confirm < block < manager_required."""
   544→    levels = {"none": 0, "confirm": 1, "block": 2, "manager_required": 3}
   545→    if levels.get(new_level, 0) > levels.get(current, 0):
   546→        return new_level
   547→    return current
   548→
   549→
   550→def _can_override_po_block():
   551→    """Check if current user has manager role for PO-stage override."""
   552→    user_roles = frappe.get_roles()
   553→    return "Component Master Manager" in user_roles or "System Manager" in user_roles
   554→
   555→
   556→def _check_procurement_blocking(project, item_code, old_bom_name):
   557→    """
   558→    Check if procurement exists and determine blocking level.
   559→
   560→    Checks children of the old BOM for active MR/RFQ/PO.
   561→
   562→    Returns:
   563→        dict: {
   564→            "can_proceed": bool,
   565→            "blocking_level": "none" | "confirm" | "block" | "manager_required",
   566→            "procurement_docs": [...],
   567→            "message": str
   568→        }
   569→    """
   570→    if not old_bom_name:
   571→        return {
   572→            "can_proceed": True,
   573→            "blocking_level": "none",
   574→            "procurement_docs": [],
   575→            "message": "No existing BOM",
   576→        }
   577→
   578→    # Get all child items from old BOM
   579→    old_bom_items = frappe.get_all(
   580→        "BOM Item",
   581→        filters={"parent": old_bom_name},
   582→        fields=["item_code"]
   583→    )
   584→
   585→    blocking_level = "none"
   586→    procurement_docs = []
   587→
   588→    # Check each child item for procurement
   589→    for bom_item in old_bom_items:
   590→        child_item = bom_item.item_code
   591→
   592→        # Check Material Requests
   593→        mrs = _get_material_requests(project, child_item)
   594→        if mrs:
   595→            for mr in mrs:
   596→                procurement_docs.append({
   597→                    "doctype": "Material Request",
   598→                    "name": mr.name,
   599→                    "status": mr.status or "Draft" if mr.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
   600→                    "quantity": mr.qty,
   601→                    "item_code": child_item,
   602→                })
   603→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "block")
   604→
   605→        # Check RFQs
   606→        rfqs = _get_rfqs(project, child_item)
   607→        if rfqs:
   608→            for rfq in rfqs:
   609→                procurement_docs.append({
   610→                    "doctype": "Request for Quotation",
   611→                    "name": rfq.name,
   612→                    "status": rfq.status or "Draft" if rfq.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
   613→                    "quantity": rfq.qty,
   614→                    "item_code": child_item,
   615→                })
   616→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "block")
   617→
   618→        # Check Purchase Orders
   619→        pos = _get_purchase_orders(project, child_item)
   620→        if pos:
   621→            for po in pos:
   622→                procurement_docs.append({
   623→                    "doctype": "Purchase Order",
   624→                    "name": po.name,
   625→                    "status": po.status or "Draft" if po.docstatus == 0 else "Submitted",
   626→                    "quantity": po.qty,
   627→                    "item_code": child_item,
   628→                })
   629→            blocking_level = _max_blocking_level(blocking_level, "manager_required")
   630→
   631→    # If no procurement found, still need confirmation for hash change
   632→    if blocking_level == "none":
   633→        blocking_level = "confirm"
   634→
   635→    # Determine if user can proceed
   636→    can_proceed = False
   637→    message = ""
   638→
   639→    if blocking_level == "confirm":
   640→        can_proceed = True
   641→        message = "BOM structure changed. Confirmation with remarks required."
   642→    elif blocking_level == "block":
   643→        can_proceed = False
   644→        message = "Cannot change BOM. Child items have active Material Requests or RFQs."
   645→    elif blocking_level == "manager_required":
   646→        if _can_override_po_block():
   647→            can_proceed = True
   648→            message = "Child items have active Purchase Orders. Manager override available."
   649→        else:
   650→            can_proceed = False
   651→            message = "Cannot change BOM. Child items have active Purchase Orders. Manager role required."
   652→
   653→    return {
   654→        "can_proceed": can_proceed,
   655→        "blocking_level": blocking_level,
   656→        "procurement_docs": procurement_docs,
   657→        "message": message,
   658→    }
   659→
   660→
   661→@frappe.whitelist()
   662→def confirm_version_change(docname, confirmations):
   663→    """
   664→    Confirm BOM version changes and proceed with upload.
   665→
   666→    Args:
   667→        docname: BOM Upload document name
   668→        confirmations: JSON string of list of dicts with item_code and remarks
   669→
   670→    Returns:
   671→        dict: Creation summary
   672→    """
   673→    if isinstance(confirmations, str):
   674→        confirmations = json.loads(confirmations)
   675→
   676→    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)
   677→    project = doc.project
   678→
   679→    # Store remarks in Component Masters
   680→    for conf in confirmations:
   681→        item_code = conf.get("item_code")
   682→        remarks = conf.get("remarks", "")
   683→
   684→        cm_name = frappe.db.get_value(
   685→            "Project Component Master",
   686→            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   687→            "name"
   688→        )
   689→
   690→        if cm_name:
   691→            frappe.db.set_value(
   692→                "Project Component Master",
   693→                cm_name,
   694→                "version_change_remarks",
   695→                remarks
   696→            )
   697→
   698→    # Re-run the upload, now allowing changed components
   699→    return _proceed_with_confirmed_changes(doc, [c["item_code"] for c in confirmations])
   700→
   701→
   702→def _proceed_with_confirmed_changes(doc, confirmed_items):
   703→    """
   704→    Proceed with BOM creation for confirmed changed items.
   705→    Deactivates old BOMs and creates new ones.
   706→    """
   707→    # Parse Excel again
   708→    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
   709→    content = file_doc.get_content()
   710→
   711→    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
   712→    ws = wb.active
   713→
   714→    rows = parse_rows(ws)
   715→    tree = build_tree(rows)
   716→
   717→    if not tree:
   718→        frappe.throw(_("No components found in the Excel file."))
   719→
   720→    project = doc.project
   721→
   722→    # Ensure items exist
   723→    ensure_items_for_all_nodes(tree)
   724→
   725→    # Deactivate old BOMs for confirmed items
   726→    for item_code in confirmed_items:
   727→        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
   728→            "Project Component Master",
   729→            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   730→            ["name", "active_bom"],
   731→            as_dict=True
   732→        )
   733→        if cm and cm.active_bom:
   734→            # Deactivate the old BOM
   735→            old_bom = frappe.get_doc("BOM", cm.active_bom)
   736→            old_bom.is_active = 0
   737→            old_bom.is_default = 0
   738→            old_bom.flags.ignore_validate = True
   739→            old_bom.save(ignore_permissions=True)
   740→
   741→            # Clear active_bom so new one can be linked
   742→            frappe.db.set_value(
   743→                "Project Component Master",
   744→                cm.name,
   745→                "active_bom",
   746→                None
   747→            )
   748→
   749→    # Build analysis with confirmed items treated as "new"
   750→    all_assemblies = _get_assembly_nodes(tree)
   751→    confirmed_set = set(confirmed_items)
   752→
   753→    can_create = []
   754→    for node in all_assemblies:
   755→        item_code = node["item_code"]
   756→
   757→        # Check if already has active BOM (unchanged)
   758→        cm = frappe.db.get_value(
   759→            "Project Component Master",
   760→            {"project": project, "item_code": item_code},
   761→            ["active_bom", "bom_structure_hash"],
   762→            as_dict=True
   763→        )
   764→
   765→        if cm and cm.active_bom:
   766→            # Already has active BOM - compare hashes
   767→            children = node.get("children", [])
   768→            new_hash = calculate_tree_structure_hash(children)
   769→            if new_hash == cm.bom_structure_hash:
   770→                continue  # Unchanged, skip
   771→
   772→        # Either new, confirmed, or needs BOM
   773→        can_create.append(node)
   774→
   775→    # Create BOMs
   776→    analysis = {
   777→        "can_create": can_create,
   778→        "changed_components": [],
   779→        "loose_blocked": [],
   780→        "blocked_by_dependencies": {},
   781→        "summary": {"can_create": len(can_create)},
   782→    }
   783→
   784→    result = create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis)
   785→
   786→    frappe.db.commit()
   787→    return result
   788→
   789→
   790→# ==================== Step 6 & 7: Create BOMs and Link ====================
   791→
   792→def create_boms_and_link_components(tree, project, analysis):
   793→    """
   794→    Create BOMs for components that passed analysis, then link active_bom
   795→    back to their Component Masters.
   796→    """
   797→    created = 0
   798→    skipped = 0
   799→    failed = 0
   800→    errors = []
   801→
   802→    # Only create BOMs for items in the can_create list
   803→    can_create_codes = {n["item_code"] for n in analysis["can_create"]}
   804→
   805→    for node in tree:
   806→        try:
   807→            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes)
   808→            created += c
   809→            skipped += s
   810→            failed += f
   811→            errors.extend(e)
   812→        except Exception as ex:
   813→            failed += 1
   814→            errors.append(f"{node['item_code']}: {str(ex)}")
   815→            frappe.log_error(
   816→                title=f"BOM creation failed for {node['item_code']}",
   817→                message=frappe.get_traceback(),
   818→            )
   819→
   820→    # Link active_bom back to Component Masters
   821→    _link_boms_to_component_masters(project)
   822→
   823→    return {
   824→        "status": "success",
   825→        "created": created,
   826→        "skipped": skipped,
   827→        "failed": failed,
   828→        "errors": errors,
   829→    }
   830→
   831→
   832→def _create_bom_for_node(node, project, can_create_codes):
   833→    """
   834→    Create BOM for a single node using existing create_bom_recursive.
   835→    Only processes nodes whose item_code is in can_create_codes.
   836→    """
   837→    created = 0
   838→    skipped = 0
   839→    failed = 0
   840→    errors = []
   841→
   842→    item_code = node["item_code"]
   843→
   844→    if not node.get("children"):
   845→        # Leaf node — no BOM needed
   846→        return created, skipped, failed, errors
   847→
   848→    if item_code not in can_create_codes:
   849→        skipped += 1
   850→        return created, skipped, failed, errors
   851→
   852→    try:
   853→        created_flag = create_bom_recursive(node, project)
   854→        if created_flag:
   855→            created += 1
   856→        else:
   857→            skipped += 1
   858→    except Exception as ex:
   859→        failed += 1
   860→        errors.append(f"{item_code}: {str(ex)}")
   861→
   862→    # Recurse into children
   863→    for child in node.get("children", []):
   864→        if child.get("children"):
   865→            c, s, f, e = _create_bom_for_node(child, project, can_create_codes)
   866→            created += c
   867→            skipped += s
   868→            failed += f
   869→            errors.extend(e)
   870→
   871→    return created, skipped, failed, errors
   872→
   873→
   874→def _link_boms_to_component_masters(project):
   875→    """
   876→    After BOMs are created, link active_bom back to Component Masters
   877→    that don't have one set yet.
   878→    """
   879→    # Find Component Masters without active_bom
   880→    cms = frappe.get_all(
   881→        "Project Component Master",
   882→        filters={
   883→            "project": project,
   884→            "has_bom": 1,
   885→            "active_bom": ("is", "not set"),
   886→        },
   887→        fields=["name", "item_code"],
   888→    )
   889→
   890→    for cm_data in cms:
   891→        # Find the active default BOM for this item
   892→        bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
   893→            "BOM",
   894→            {
   895→                "item": cm_data.item_code,
   896→                "project": project,
   897→                "is_active": 1,
   898→                "is_default": 1,
   899→                "docstatus": 1,
   900→            },
   901→            "name",
   902→        )
   903→
   904→        if not bom_name:
   905→            # Also check without project filter (BOM may not have project set)
   906→            bom_name = frappe.db.get_value(
   907→                "BOM",
   908→                {
   909→                    "item": cm_data.item_code,
   910→                    "is_active": 1,
   911→                    "is_default": 1,
   912→                    "docstatus": 1,
   913→                },
   914→                "name",
   915→            )
   916→
   917→        if bom_name:
   918→            cm = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm_data.name)
   919→            cm.active_bom = bom_name
   920→
   921→            # Recalculate hash from actual BOM doc
   922→            try:
   923→                bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name)
   924→                cm.bom_structure_hash = calculate_bom_doc_hash(bom_doc)
   925→            except Exception:
   926→                pass
   927→
   928→            cm.flags.ignore_validate = True
   929→            cm.flags.ignore_mandatory = True
   930→            cm.save(ignore_permissions=True)
   931→
   932→
   933→# ==================== Hash Calculation ====================
   934→
   935→def calculate_tree_structure_hash(children):
   936→    """
   937→    Create hash of BOM structure from tree node children (parsed from Excel).
   938→    Uses same logic as bom_hooks.calculate_bom_structure_hash but works
   939→    on tree node dicts instead of BOM doc items.
   940→
   941→    Args:
   942→        children: List of child node dicts with item_code and qty
   943→
   944→    Returns:
   945→        str: MD5 hash of sorted (item_code, qty) tuples
   946→    """
   947→    if not children:
   948→        return None
   949→
   950→    structure = sorted(
   951→        [(child["item_code"], float(child.get("qty", 1))) for child in children],
   952→        key=lambda x: x[0],
   953→    )
   954→    structure_str = json.dumps(structure, sort_keys=True)
   955→    return hashlib.md5(structure_str.encode()).hexdigest()
   956→
   957→
   958→# ==================== Serialization ====================
   959→
   960→def _serialize_analysis(analysis):
   961→    """
   962→    Serialize analysis dict for JSON response to client.
   963→    Convert node dicts to safe serializable format.
   964→    """
   965→    def serialize_node(node):
   966→        return {
   967→            "item_code": node.get("item_code"),
   968→            "description": node.get("description"),
   969→            "level": node.get("level"),
   970→            "qty": node.get("qty"),
   971→        }
   972→
   973→    def serialize_details(details):
   974→        """Serialize details dict, ensuring procurement_docs is serializable."""
   975→        if not details:
   976→            return {}
   977→        return {
   978→            "change_type": details.get("change_type"),
   979→            "component_master": details.get("component_master"),
   980→            "old_bom": details.get("old_bom"),
   981→            "blocking_level": details.get("blocking_level"),
   982→            "blocking_message": details.get("blocking_message"),
   983→            "can_proceed": details.get("can_proceed"),
   984→            "procurement_docs": details.get("procurement_docs", []),
   985→        }
   986→
   987→    serialized = {
   988→        "can_create": [serialize_node(n) for n in analysis.get("can_create", [])],
   989→        "changed_components": [],
   990→        "loose_blocked": [],
   991→        "blocked_by_dependencies": analysis.get("blocked_by_dependencies", {}),
   992→        "summary": analysis.get("summary", {}),
   993→    }
   994→
   995→    for comp in analysis.get("changed_components", []):
   996→        serialized["changed_components"].append({
   997→            "node": serialize_node(comp.get("node", {})),
   998→            "details": serialize_details(comp.get("details", {})),
   999→        })
  1000→
  1001→    for comp in analysis.get("loose_blocked", []):
  1002→        serialized["loose_blocked"].append({
  1003→            "node": serialize_node(comp.get("node", {})),
  1004→            "details": comp.get("details", {}),
  1005→        })
  1006→
  1007→    return serialized
  1008→
